svg.title: The request and response loop: every message is a frame of JSON over HTTP
svg.desc: Section one shows the request frame: headers plus a payload of the question and optional data you provide. Section two shows the app sending that frame as JSON over HTTP to the model's data center. Section three shows the response, which is also a frame: a green response frame carrying the model's reply for HTTP 200, or a red response frame carrying an error object for any other status code.
svg.text: Every message is a FRAME — JSON over HTTP, both ways
1. The REQUEST frame — what you send
Note: there is NO reply in the request frame — the reply does not exist yet. It is created by the model.
2. Your app SENDS the request frame — JSON over HTTP
3. The data center RESPONDS — the response is ALSO a frame
The model ran. Its answer is the
RED response frame — any other code
No reply — the model did not run.
The payload is an error instead.
Request frame goes out (question + your data). A response frame comes back — same JSON-over-HTTP shape.
Only the PAYLOAD differs: green frame carries the reply, red frame carries an error. Always check the status code.
